history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade due to type mismatches (LocalDateTime vs Date) and method signature changes in custom Spring Batch integration code.
history.qwen_rationale: The UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe handles framework-level migrations but may miss custom code adaptations. Adding SpringBoot3BestPractices and explicitly running JavaxMigrationToJakarta in the same step increases the chance of resolving annotation/config-driven type issues and ensures all javax->jakarta transitions are applied before compilation.

history.qwen_observation: Recipe validation failed because org.openrewrite.java.spring.boot3.SpringBoot3BestPractices does not exist in the OpenRewrite registry.
history.qwen_rationale: Removed the non-existent SpringBoot3BestPractices recipe from the upgrade_spring_boot_3_3 step. The chain now relies on the valid UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe combined with JavaxMigrationToJakarta to handle framework and namespace transitions, followed by the standard Java 17 and 21 transformation steps.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade due to type mismatches (LocalDateTime vs Date) and method signature changes in Spring Batch-related classes.
history.qwen_rationale: The UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe updates dependencies and code, but may leave custom application code incompatible with Spring Batch 5 APIs. Replacing it with UpgradeSpringBoot_3_0 reduces the scope of transitive dependency bumps while still performing the critical javax-to-jakarta migration, potentially avoiding the aggressive API changes that broke the build.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails during Spring Boot 3 upgrade due to custom code incompatibilities (Spring Batch API signature changes and LocalDateTime/Date type mismatches) that OpenRewrite recipes do not automatically resolve.
history.qwen_rationale: Following the empirical template for Spring Boot 2.x to 3.x migrations, the explicit Java 17 build upgrade step is replaced with the UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe, which internally handles the JDK 17 transition, dependency bumps, and Jakarta namespace migration. This aligns with the recommended path for SB projects, though custom source code adjustments for framework API changes remain outside the scope of automated recipes.

history.qwen_observation: The direct jump to Spring Boot 3.3 caused compilation failures due to API changes in Spring Batch 5 and Spring Data 3, specifically type mismatches (LocalDateTime vs Date) and method signature changes in custom controllers/services.
history.qwen_rationale: Incrementally upgrading Spring Boot from 2.7 to 3.0, then 3.1, 3.2, and finally 3.3 allows OpenRewrite to apply smaller, more targeted changes per minor version, reducing the risk of cascading API incompatibilities and giving the build system a chance to stabilize between major framework shifts.
